skill.name: seedance-prompt
skill.description: Creates structured Seedance 2.0 motion graphics prompts for 15-second product videos, including style selection, reference image guidance, sound design, music direction, and optional Dreamina CLI handoff.
skill.summary: Generate Seedance 2.0 product video prompts with motion design structure and reference image guidance.
skill.author: op7418

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and blocker patterns. Most Ruby backtick, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and high entropy alerts are false positives from Markdown code formatting, the .md extension, and prompt-template text. The confirmed risk is an optional workflow that may run local commands and install a third-party CLI through curl piped to bash, so publication should wait for safer installation guidance.

high_findings.title: Remote Installer Piped To Shell
high_findings.description: The skill and README recommend installing Dreamina CLI with curl piped directly to bash. This executes a remote script without an integrity check, pinned version, or visible review step.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell command is present in both the skill instructions and documentation. The context confirms it is an install path for a third-party CLI, not a harmless text example.

medium_findings.title: Assistant-Directed Local Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run which dreamina or dreamina --version, then optionally use the Dreamina CLI with generated prompts and image paths. This is legitimate functionality, but it expands execution and data-sharing risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly described and tied to the skill workflow. User consent is requested, so the risk is elevated but not confirmed malicious.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Path Handling For User Images
medium_findings.description: The skill asks users for image paths and passes those paths to the CLI when reference images are used. This may expose local file paths or unintended image files to an external video service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path flow is directly documented, but no automatic exfiltration is shown. The concern depends on how the assistant and CLI handle user-provided paths.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Service Links
low_findings.description: The skill documentation links to Seedance, Dreamina CLI, GitHub, and an X post. These links are expected for this workflow, but users should know the skill depends on external services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links or service endpoints. They do not show hidden exfiltration, but they confirm external dependency risk.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives From Markdown
low_findings.description: Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and entropy alerts appear to come from Markdown code spans, prompt examples, md file names, and ordinary descriptive text. No evidence found of hidden binary payloads or cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows prompt templates and Markdown formatting rather than executable source code. The only confirmed executable concern is documented separately.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl Piped To bash
dangerous_patterns.description: The command downloads an installer from a remote URL and executes it immediately in the shell. This pattern can run arbitrary code if the endpoint or network path is compromised.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is present verbatim and matches a known dangerous installation pattern. The surrounding text confirms it is intended to be executed.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the assistant to detect and use Dreamina CLI after generating a prompt. External CLI execution can affect the local machine and may upload prompts or image assets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly calls for command checks and later CLI use. The exact CLI generation command is not shown, which keeps confidence below very high.

content.value_statement: Product video prompts are hard to structure for motion, timing, and visual continuity. This skill guides Seedance 2.0 prompts with clear stages, style choices, audio notes, and reference image placement.
actual_capabilities: Creates 15-second one-take Seedance 2.0 motion graphics prompts.
Selects from Apple, Microsoft, Bauhaus, and Vercel-inspired visual styles.
Adds timestamped stages with morphing transitions and logo reveal timing.
Includes sound design, music direction, and optional voiceover placement.
Marks reference image placeholders and explains their intended use.
Can guide optional Dreamina CLI generation after checking installation.
limitations: It creates prompts and guidance, not finished videos by itself.
Dreamina CLI installation uses a high-risk remote shell installer.
Generated quality depends on product details and image assets supplied by the user.
It targets Seedance 2.0 and may not fit other video models without edits.

use_cases.title: Launch Prompt For Founders
use_cases.description: Create a polished short product video prompt from a product summary, logo, and launch message.

use_cases.title: Motion Direction For Designers
use_cases.description: Translate brand style, interface screenshots, and feature ideas into a timed motion graphics sequence.

use_cases.title: Campaign Assets For Marketers
use_cases.description: Draft repeatable Seedance prompts for feature announcements, social ads, and product teasers.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a simple product video concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Seedance 2.0 product video prompt for my product. Product name: [name]. Product function: [function]. Main message: [message]. No voiceover.

prompt_templates.title: Prompt With Reference Images
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the user has a logo, product image, or screenshot.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Seedance 2.0 prompt using these reference assets: [logo path or description], [screenshot path or description]. Mark each image placeholder and explain where it appears.

prompt_templates.title: Style And Voiceover Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when brand tone and spoken copy matter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 15-second Seedance prompt for [product]. Use the [Apple, Microsoft, Bauhaus, or Vercel] style. Add short voiceover lines in [language] with a [tone] voice.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Launch Video Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt with product positioning, assets, and conversion goal.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete Seedance 2.0 launch video prompt for [product]. Audience: [audience]. Core features: [features]. Brand colors: [colors]. Assets: [assets]. Goal: [conversion goal]. Include timing, motion transitions, sound design, and music.

output_examples.input: A developer tool needs a 15-second product teaser with no voiceover.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a dark technical style prompt with six timed stages, geometric morphs, sound design, and a final logo reveal.

output_examples.input: A productivity app has a logo and three interface screenshots.
output_examples.output: The skill maps each image to a reference placeholder and explains which timeline moments should use each asset.

output_examples.input: A premium hardware product needs a launch video with a short English voiceover.
output_examples.output: The skill selects a polished glass-and-metal style, embeds brief voiceover lines, and keeps the prompt within the target length.

best_practices: Provide product positioning, target audience, key features, and brand tone before generation.
Use clear logo and interface images when asking for reference image guidance.
Review any Dreamina CLI command before execution and prefer verified installation methods.
anti_patterns: Do not run the remote installer without reviewing the source and trust boundary.
Do not provide private screenshots or unreleased assets unless the external service is approved.
Do not reuse the generated prompt for unrelated video models without adapting timing and constraints.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured Seedance 2.0 prompts for 15-second product motion graphics videos.

faq.question: Does it generate the video file?
faq.answer: No. It writes the prompt and can guide optional Dreamina CLI use after user approval.

faq.question: Can it use product images?
faq.answer: Yes. It can label reference image placeholders and explain where each image should appear.

faq.question: Which styles are supported?
faq.answer: It supports Apple, Microsoft, Bauhaus, and Vercel-inspired motion design styles.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: The skill includes a curl pipe to bash installer and local CLI execution guidance.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
